data.title: Bitcoin Adoption May Rise as Confidence in Fiat Declines, Says Tim Draper
data.description:
Silicon Valley investor and Bitcoin advocate Tim Draper recently shared his views on the potential shift from fiat currency to Bitcoin. According to Foresight News, Draper expressed these thoughts during an appearance on 'TheStreet Roundtable.' He suggested that diminishing confidence in fiat currencies could eventually lead businesses and consumers to adopt Bitcoin.

Draper highlighted the devaluation of the dollar, noting that people might become frustrated as its value drops from one dollar to 90 cents, 80 cents, 70 cents, and eventually plummets rapidly towards zero. He believes that when this critical point is reached, businesses may quickly reconsider their payment methods.

As an example, Draper mentioned that if he were a retailer, his first action would be to display a sign indicating that his store accepts Bitcoin. This shift, he argues, could be a response to the declining trust in traditional currencies.

data.title: Coinbase is in talks with Bybit about an investment partnership, which could help it advance its compliant market strategy in the United States.
data.description: Coinbase is in talks with Bybit about an investment and partnership agreement, with Bybit hoping to gain access to the compliant US market. Reports indicate that ICE previously invested in offshore exchange OKX at a valuation of approximately $25 billion, and the market expects Bybit's valuation to be similar. (Wu Shuo)

data.title: Nigel Farage Faces New Challenges as Rivals Target His Support Base
data.description: Nigel Farage has sparked a crisis within mainstream UK conservatism by attracting voters from its right-wing fringe. Bloomberg posted on X, after a year of leading the polls, Farage now faces challenges as others adopt similar strategies to target his support base. This shift in tactics highlights the evolving dynamics within the UK's political landscape, as parties vie for influence and voter support.

data.title: Bitcoin Exchange Holdings Reach Lowest Level Since November
data.description: Santiment posted on X that the percentage of Bitcoin held on exchanges has decreased to its lowest point since November. This trend indicates a shift in investor behavior, as more individuals are opting to store their Bitcoin in private wallets rather than on exchanges. The decline in exchange holdings suggests a growing confidence in the long-term value of Bitcoin, as investors are less inclined to sell their assets. This development is significant in the context of the cryptocurrency market, where exchange holdings are often used as a measure of market sentiment and liquidity. The reduction in Bitcoin on exchanges could potentially lead to increased price stability and reduced volatility, as fewer coins are available for trading. This trend is being closely monitored by analysts and investors alike, as it may have implications for future market movements and investment strategies.

data.title: Russia Criticizes U.S. and Israel's Failed Strategy Against Iran
data.description: On March 13, local time, Russia's Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Vassily Nebenzia, stated in an interview with Russia Today that the 'blitzkrieg' by the United States and Israel against Iran has failed. According to Jin10, Nebenzia noted that the Iranian government did not collapse, there was no uprising among the populace, and the U.S. has not abandoned its strategy in this operation. He further highlighted that the U.S. and Israel recently admitted to having premeditated actions against Iran, planning military operations during U.S.-Iran negotiations and conspiring to provoke incidents two to three months ago. In contrast, Iran has consistently shown sincerity in advancing negotiations with the U.S.

data.title: North Korea Launches Multiple Missiles into Nearby Waters
data.description: North Korea has reportedly launched ten ballistic missiles into the waters near the East Sea, according to South Korean military sources. Earlier reports from South Korea indicated that North Korea had fired an unidentified flying object, which the Japanese government suggested might be a ballistic missile. According to RTHK, Japan's Maritime Self-Defense Force noted that the missile might have fallen into the sea. Additionally, Japan Broadcasting Corporation reported that the missiles appeared to have landed outside Japan's economic zone.

data.title: Proposed CLARITY Act Aims to Address Stablecoin Issues
data.description: House Financial Services Committee Chairman French Hill has announced that the proposed CLARITY Act could provide solutions to unresolved stablecoin issues left by the GENIUS Act. According to NS3.AI, Hill stated that lawmakers have reached a bipartisan consensus that stablecoins should not offer interest payments.

data.title: Pharos receives strategic investment from GCL New Energy, valuing the company at nearly $1 billion.
data.description: Layer 1 blockchain project Pharos has secured strategic investment from GCL New Energy, valuing the project at nearly $1 billion. This investment, previously announced on January 8, 2026, was finalized after completing relevant disclosure procedures with the Hong Kong Stock Exchange (HKEX). The report indicates that the collaboration will focus on applications related to real-world assets (RWA), including renewable energy asset tokenization, decentralized energy trading systems, and carbon footprint tracking and verification. Pharos states that its parallel execution architecture is designed to support high-throughput financial infrastructure, improving asset settlement efficiency, transparency, and liquidity. GCL New Energy is a Hong Kong-listed energy company, and the two companies plan to explore the application of blockchain technology in energy infrastructure and financial markets through this collaboration. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Alchemy launches AgentCard, enabling AI Agents to support features such as Polymarket transactions and food delivery shopping.
data.description: According to official news, Alchemy's AI-powered agent payment platform, AgentCard, has officially launched. Users' AI agents can now: • Pay for inference and APIs; • Order food through DoorDash, Amazon, and Uber; • Handle marketing; • Conduct transactions on Polymarket 24/7. It is understood that the product is open to everyone, not just businesses; and features instant access, privacy, and reusability. Notably, this news has been officially shared and confirmed by Solana, Base, and Polymarket.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Microsoft Tests OpenClaw for Teams Integration
data.description: Microsoft is currently conducting internal tests on OpenClaw for potential integration with its Teams platform. According to NS3.AI, a roadmap detailing this integration is anticipated to be released next week. OpenClaw's founder, Peter Steinberger, shared a post from Digital Meld CEO Brad Groux, which hinted at forthcoming changes, although specific details were not disclosed.

data.title: Tibet's Government-in-Exile Monitors China's Potential Moves Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: The leader of Tibet's government-in-exile has expressed concerns about China's potential actions in the region as the conflict in Iran intensifies. Bloomberg posted on X that the administration is vigilant about whether the situation might allow China to expand its influence on the Himalayan plateau and other regional areas of interest. The ongoing tensions in Iran could provide China with opportunities to strengthen its strategic position in these sensitive regions. The Tibetan administration remains watchful of any developments that might impact the geopolitical landscape.

data.title: CZ: Block explorers should filter spam transactions to reduce the risk of address poisoning.
data.description: CZ stated in an article on the X platform that block explorers should directly filter spam transactions instead of displaying them to users to reduce the risk of address poisoning attacks. He pointed out that such transactions are technically easy to completely filter out, and some wallet applications have already implemented similar functions; for example, Trust Wallet has already filtered out relevant spam transactions. CZ also stated that filtering spam transactions may have some impact on micropayment transactions between AI agents in the future, but at that time, spam transactions can also be identified and filtered using AI technology. Previously, Etherscan warned that address poisoning attacks on Ethereum are becoming more automated and widespread, with attackers typically inducing users to mistakenly transfer funds by forging addresses that resemble real addresses.

data.title: Microsoft is internally testing OpenClaw, which may be integrated into Teams applications. A roadmap is expected to be released next week.
data.description: Peter Steinberger, founder of OpenClaw, retweeted a tweet from Brad Groux, CEO of Digital Meld, stating that OpenClaw for Microsoft Teams will receive significant improvements. A roadmap is expected to be released next week. He has already spoken with more than a dozen Microsoft employees and has formed a six-person team to internally test OpenClaw. He expects improvements to Microsoft Teams and other extensions and plugins.

data.title: Planet Midday News
data.description: 1. Coinbase Institutional: Bitcoin outperforms US stocks, crypto market may be gradually emerging from "extreme pessimism"; 2. Polymarket's US platform reported over $750 million in nominal trading volume and over 5 million transactions; 3. Paradigm releases its first prediction market poll, showing approximately 36% of US voters are now prediction market users; 4. Kalshi's weekly transaction volume surpasses 20 million for the first time, setting a new platform record; 5. The Trump administration has initiated the release process of US oil reserves; 6. Circle CEO: USYC has become the world's largest tokenized money market fund; 7. POAP platform will enter maintenance mode on March 16th, unable to provide badge issuance functionality, existing badge collections will not be affected; 8. US military officials reveal the war may continue for several more weeks, Iran calls Hargh Island a red line; 9. Relatives of the "Fujian gang" in Singapore's largest money laundering case have their London assets, worth approximately 55 million RMB, to be seized by the UK.

data.title: Data: Only 77 ships have passed through the Strait of Hormuz since March 1st.
data.description: The conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran has caused a blockage in the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global energy transport route. Maritime data shows that only 77 ships have passed through the Strait of Hormuz since March 1st. In comparison, 1,229 ships passed through the strait between March 1st and 11th last year. AFP, citing Lloyd's of London Marine Information Service on the 13th, reported that most of the 77 ships that passed through were old and in poor condition, lacked proper insurance, and had unclear ownership. The agency, which provides maritime data and analysis services, stated that most of these ships belong to a so-called "shadow fleet," namely oil tankers and LNG carriers circumventing Western sanctions. (Jinshi)

data.title: Chairman of the U.S. House Financial Services Committee: The Clarity Act may fill the regulatory gap left by the GENIUS Act.
data.description: French Hill, chairman of the House Financial Services Committee, stated that the CLARITY Act is expected to address some regulatory issues not covered by the GENIUS Act. In an interview, Hill noted that the banking industry remains concerned about the proposed crypto regulatory framework, particularly regarding the potential regulatory treatment of crypto companies. Hill pointed out that the House passed the CLARITY Act last summer with bipartisan support, including 78 Democratic votes. This bill is part of Washington's push to establish a regulatory system for digital assets, aiming to clarify how stablecoins and other digital assets operate in the U.S. financial market. Furthermore, policymakers are discussing whether crypto companies should be subject to the same level of regulation as banks. Hill stated that both parties have reached a consensus on a key principle: stablecoins should not pay returns to holders, an issue central to the discussions surrounding the GENIUS Act.

data.title: BlackRock's Strategic Approach to Crypto ETFs Amid New Launches
data.description:
BlackRock, the $14 trillion asset management giant, is maintaining a cautious strategy in its approach to cryptocurrency exchange-traded funds (ETFs), despite launching a staking-focused Ether ETF. According to Cointelegraph, Robert Mitchnick, BlackRock’s head of digital assets, emphasized that while other asset managers are exploring innovative crypto ETF structures, BlackRock will continue to adopt a more measured approach. Speaking on CNBC’s Crypto World segment, Mitchnick acknowledged the potential appeal of these novel structures to certain investors but reiterated BlackRock's commitment to a discerning strategy.

Mitchnick noted that while Bitcoin (BTC) and Ether (ETH) remain the primary focus of investor interest, there is also growing curiosity about other digital assets. BlackRock is evaluating these interests as market conditions evolve, considering factors such as maturity, liquidity, and use cases. The company aims to be selective about the assets included in its iShares ETFs. The recent launch of the iShares Staked Ethereum Trust (ETHB) saw significant trading activity, with over $15.5 million in trading volume and $43.5 million in inflows on its debut. ETHB offers investors the opportunity to earn yield through Ethereum staking rewards, in addition to potential price gains.

BlackRock's ETHB is the firm's second Ether-focused product, following the iShares Ethereum Trust ETF (ETHA), which has attracted nearly $12 billion in inflows since its launch in July 2024. Additionally, BlackRock is developing a Bitcoin Premium Income ETF, which will involve selling covered call options on Bitcoin futures to generate yield through premiums. However, this strategy may limit potential upside gains from investing in BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust ETH (IBIT), which tracks Bitcoin’s spot price.

Mitchnick highlighted that investors in BlackRock’s flagship Bitcoin product, IBIT, have predominantly been long-term holders, even amid significant selling pressure in the broader Bitcoin market. These investors have shown a tendency to buy during market dips, contributing to the over $63 billion in inflows IBIT has received since its launch in January 2024. This strategic approach underscores BlackRock's focus on stability and long-term growth in the evolving crypto ETF landscape.

data.title: WLFI: Locking up $5 million worth of tokens will grant you access to team communication opportunities.
data.description: World Liberty Financial (WLFI), a crypto project with ties to the Trump family, stated on its website that investors who lock up approximately $5 million worth of WLFI tokens for six months will gain opportunities to communicate with the project's business development team and executives, as well as governance voting rights. The project refers to holders of approximately 50 million WLFI tokens as "Super Nodes." World Liberty filings show that its "support team" includes Eric Trump, Donald Trump Jr., and Barron Trump; however, the company stated that current President Trump and his family members will not participate in these communication arrangements. (Reuters)

data.title: JERA CEO: Middle East Tensions Unlikely to Impact LNG Supply Immediately
data.description: JERA, Japan's largest power company, has stated that despite ongoing tensions in the Middle East, there is no immediate threat to the supply of liquefied natural gas (LNG). According to Jin10, the company's global CEO, Kani, reassured stakeholders that current geopolitical issues are not expected to disrupt LNG availability in the near term. This statement comes amid concerns over potential supply chain disruptions due to regional instability. JERA continues to monitor the situation closely to ensure energy security and maintain steady operations.

data.title: Energy Conservation Measures Considered Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: Japan's largest power company, JERA, is evaluating potential energy conservation strategies in response to ongoing Middle East tensions. According to Jin10, JERA's global CEO, Kane, stated that if the conflict persists, collaboration with the government may be necessary to implement measures such as promoting electricity savings and restarting idle coal power plants. These steps aim to mitigate potential disruptions in energy supply and ensure stability in the face of geopolitical uncertainties.
